growth_records.medium: Marine Basal Medium + 3 mM ammonium + phytoplankton metabolite (12 mM carbon)

up.units: acts as growth substrate (significant growth within 24 h); numeric max growth rate in Dataset S1

growth_records.cite: Clerc et al. Chemotaxis, growth, and inter-species interactions shape early bacterial community assembly. ISME J (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf101. [PMC12202767]
growth_records.snippet: resuspended to an OD600 of ~0.01 in Marine Basal Medium supplemented with 3 mM ammonium and each phytoplankton metabolite at 12 mM carbon. Cultures were grown in a 96-well plate in triplicate ... at 30 C with constant shaking and OD600 was
growth_records.notes: Marine copiotroph growth assay. NO numeric growth rates in the text; all maximum growth rate values are reported only in Dataset S1 (xlsx) and Figs 1/4, which are not machine-readable here, so growth_rate_per_h is null (not invented). Text states qualitatively that DMSP is a growth substrate for Thalassospira sp. HF15 (dagger = significant growth within 24 h, Fig 1A). Base Marine Basal Medium composition cited (ref 42) not itemized in text -> medium_composition_stated=false; only ammonium 3 mM and metabolite 12 mM-carbon supplements are explicit. Metabolites tested as sole C source: DMSP, spermidine, ectoine, DHPS, TMA, glycolate, ribose, isethionate, xylose, glutamate. No arithmetic performed.

growth_records.medium: Defined artificial seawater (ESAW, modified) with P. tricornutum exudates + 13C/15N-labeled algal DOM

up.method: nanoSIMS single-cell 13C net incorporation scaled by cell size and abundance
nanoSIMS single-cell 15N net incorporation scaled by cell size and abundance

growth_records.cite: Mayali et al. Single-cell isotope tracing reveals functional guilds of bacteria associated with the diatom Phaeodactylum tricornutum. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-41179-9. [PMC10499878]
growth_records.snippet: Thalassospira 13M1/11-3 ... Nnet CD 30%, Cnet CD 36%, µg C from DOC 1.2, µg N from DON 0.1 (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 volumetric net uptake: DOC 1.2 µg C L-1 d-1, DON 0.1 µg N L-1 d-1 over 24 h. Abundance 1.70E6 cells/mL. Cnet CD 36%, Nnet CD 30%. Small-molecule user; high glycolate incorporator (2-day Cnet >5%). No µ.
